question: How do you configure Pebble log forwarding to Loki? Show the layer YAML and explain the protocol options.

claude_output.result:
## Pebble Log Forwarding to Loki

### Layer YAML

```yaml
log-targets:
  loki-receiver:
    override: merge
    type: loki
    location: http://<loki-host>:3100/loki/api/v1/push
    services: [all]
    labels:
      environment: production
      version: $APP_VERSION
```

### Key fields

| Field | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `type` | Yes | `loki`, `opentelemetry`, or `syslog` |
| `location` | Yes | Full URL to the push endpoint |
| `services` | No | List of service names, or `[all]`; prefix with `-` to exclude |
| `labels` | No | Arbitrary key/value pairs; values support `$ENV_VAR` substitution |
| `override` | No | `merge` (default) or `replace` |

### Protocol options

**`loki`** — Grafana Loki push API
- Endpoint: `http://<host>:3100/loki/api/v1/push`
- Pebble automatically adds a `pebble_service` label

**`opentelemetry`** — OTLP over HTTP
- Endpoint: `http://<host>:4318`
- Pebble automatically adds a `service.name` label

**`syslog`** — RFC 5424 syslog
- Endpoint: `tcp://<host>:<port>` or `udp://<host>:<port>`
- SD-ID is set to `pebble@28978`; APP-NAME is set to the service name

### Services list behaviour

```yaml
services: [svc1, svc2]   # only these services
services: [all]           # every service
services: [all, -svc3]    # all except svc3
```

When `override: merge`, service lists are *appended* rather than replaced — useful when multiple charm layers each add their own services to a shared target.

### In a Kubernetes charm

In practice you'd push this layer dynamically once you have the Loki URL (typically from a relation), using the Ops/Pebble Python API:

```python
container.add_layer("loki", {
    "log-targets": {
        "loki-receiver": {
            "override": "merge",
            "type": "loki",
            "location": f"http://{loki_host}:3100/loki/api/v1/push",
            "services": ["all"],
            "labels": {"juju_application": self.app.name},
        }
    }
}, combine=True)
```
